layout_strategies.mobile_first: stack vertically on mobile, grid on tablet+

elements: Subtext: 'Trusted guidance for every stage of your dog's life.'

CoreActionBuckets.style: Bento Grid style, full height cards

LifeStageSection.card_style: Visual heavy, text overlay or bottom caption

ProductSection.title: Tools for Learning
ProductSection.style: Clean, minimal, no border, focus on image

ServiceSection.title: Expert Services
ServiceSection.style: Watercolor Illustration style (simulated via CSS or placeholders)
ServiceSection.instruction: Use soft, organic shapes and pastel colors to mimic watercolor if assets are missing. Use CSS blend modes.

accessibility.contrast: Ensure text-gray-500 is readable on colored backgrounds. Use text-gray-700 for better contrast.

instructions_to_main_agent: Implement the 'Ask Mira' input as the main hero element.
Create the 3-bucket Core Action Cards section using the new 'Bento Grid' style.
Use the provided image URLs for Life Stage and Product sections to replace generic stock photos.
For Services, use the provided watercolor-style images or apply a 'mix-blend-multiply' effect on white backgrounds to simulate the look.
Ensure the 'Near Me' component is included.
Separate Products and Services into distinct, high-quality sections.
Make the page feel 'Soulful' - soft backgrounds, rounded corners (xl or 2xl), generous padding.
UNIVERSAL GUIDELINES FOR MAIN AGENT: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur)
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: transition: all. This results in breaking transforms. Always add transitions for specific interactive elements like button, input excluding transforms
You MUST NOT center align the app container, ie do not add .App { text-align: center; } in the css file. This disrupts the human natural reading flow of text
Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style
NEVER: use AI assistant Emoji characters like 🤖🧠💭💡🔮🎯📚🎭🎬🎪🎉🎊🎁🎀🎂🍰🎈🎨🎰💰💵💳🏦💎🪙💸🤑📊📈📉💹🔢🏆🥇 etc for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, parallax effects, and entrance animations. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Subtle grain textures, noise overlays, custom cursors, selection states, and loading animations: separates good from extraordinary.
Main agent relies on these image URLs. Missing categories (especially sponsor logos) = main agent will use placeholder text which is FORBIDDEN

Component Reuse: Prioritize using pre-existing components from src/components/ui when applicable. Create new components that match the style and conventions of existing components when needed. Examine existing components to understand the project's component patterns before creating new ones
Export Conventions: Components MUST use named exports (export const ComponentName = ...). Pages MUST use default exports (export default function PageName() {...})
Toasts: Use sonner for toasts. Sonner component are located in /app/src/components/ui/sonner.tsx
